reasoning: The informal statement claims there is no order on ℂ making it an ordered field. In modern Mathlib, an ordered field is encoded as `[Field K] [LinearOrder K] [IsStrictOrderedRing K]` (confirmed by the deprecation notice in `Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Field/Defs.lean`). Since ℂ already has a Field instance globally, the Lean statement `¬ (∃ inst : LinearOrder ℂ, IsStrictOrderedRing ℂ)` asserts exactly that no linear order can be put on ℂ that would make it a strict ordered ring — i.e., that makes it an ordered field. This captures the ordered field claim faithfully, including the requirement that the order be a total/linear order (as ordered fields in Rudin are total orders). The quantifier structure and mathematical content match precisely.
